seoTitle: Will Semiconductor Products | CMOS Sensor Power Management | BeiLuo Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Will Semiconductor CMOS image sensors, power management ICs, signal chain products. Authorized distributor with technical support and inventory.

faqs.question: What product categories does Will Semiconductor offer?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers four main product categories: CMOS Image Sensors for mobile, automotive, security, and industrial imaging applications; Power Management ICs including DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, battery management solutions, and LED drivers; Signal Chain Products featuring analog switches, interface ICs, operational amplifiers, and level translators; and RF/Connectivity solutions including RF front-end modules and Bluetooth Low Energy devices. Each category includes multiple product families targeting specific applications and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our product categories to find detailed specifications, or contact our FAE team for product recommendations based on your specific application requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right CMOS image sensor for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right CMOS image sensor requires considering several key parameters: resolution requirements based on your image quality needs; pixel size affecting low-light performance; optical format determining lens compatibility; frame rate for video applications; interface type (MIPI CSI-2, DVP, etc.); and special features like HDR, global shutter, or LED flicker mitigation. For automotive applications, ensure AEC-Q100 qualification. For mobile applications, consider autofocus support and compact form factors. Our FAE team can help analyze your requirements and recommend optimal sensors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use our CMOS sensor selection guide or contact our imaging specialists for personalized recommendations based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What power management solutions does Will Semiconductor provide?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor provides comprehensive power management solutions including DC-DC buck/boost converters for efficient voltage regulation; low-dropout (LDO) linear regulators for low-noise applications; battery management ICs for charging and protection; LED drivers for display and lighting applications; and power switches and load switches for power distribution. These products support input voltages from 1.8V to 60V and output currents up to 10A, suitable for portable devices, industrial equipment, and automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our Power Management ICs category or contact our power management team for architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: How can BeiLuo help with Will Semiconductor product selection?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive product selection support for Will Semiconductor products including technical consultation with our experienced FAE team; schematic and layout review for your designs; reference designs and evaluation kits for rapid prototyping; sample provision for testing and validation; and long-term supply planning for production programs. We maintain technical documentation, application notes, and can provide factory-direct support for complex requirements. Our close relationship with Will Semiconductor ensures access to the latest products and technical resources.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical team with your project requirements for personalized product recommendations and design support.

faqs.question: What is the difference between OmniVision and Will Semiconductor products?
faqs.answer: OmniVision Technologies was acquired by Will Semiconductor in 2019. Products previously marketed under the OmniVision brand are now part of Will Semiconductor's portfolio. The product numbering system remains consistent - sensors with OV prefix are Will Semiconductor products. The acquisition combined OmniVision's advanced CMOS imaging technology with Will Semiconductor's power management and signal chain expertise. All new products are released under the Will Semiconductor brand while maintaining the technical excellence and innovation that OmniVision was known for.
faqs.decisionGuide: Products marked OmniVision or OV are available through BeiLuo as authorized Will Semiconductor distributor. Contact us for cross-references and availability.

categories.name: CMOS Image Sensors
categories.description: High-performance CMOS image sensors featuring advanced pixel technologies for mobile, automotive, security, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Will Semiconductor's CMOS image sensor portfolio, developed through OmniVision technology leadership, offers industry-leading imaging solutions for diverse applications. The product range includes high-resolution sensors up to 200MP for mobile devices, automotive-grade sensors with AEC-Q100 qualification for ADAS and surround view systems, security sensors with excellent low-light performance, and industrial sensors for machine vision. Advanced technologies include PureCel for superior image quality, Nyxel for near-infrared sensitivity, and HDR for challenging lighting conditions. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support including lens selection guidance, image tuning assistance, and reference designs for camera module integration.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Will Semiconductor CMOS Image Sensors
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for choosing the right image sensor for your application

faqs.question: What resolution options are available for Will Semiconductor CMOS sensors?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers CMOS image sensors ranging from 0.3MP (VGA) to 200MP, covering virtually all application requirements. Mobile sensors typically range from 8MP to 200MP for high-resolution photography. Automotive sensors range from 1MP to 8MP optimized for ADAS applications. Security sensors range from 2MP to 8MP with excellent low-light performance. Industrial sensors range from 1MP to 50MP for machine vision applications. The portfolio includes various aspect ratios including 4:3, 16:9, and 1:1 to suit different display and processing requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your resolution requirements based on image quality needs and processing capabilities. Contact us for sensor recommendations.

faqs.question: What is PureCel technology and what are its benefits?
faqs.answer: PureCel is Will Semiconductor's advanced pixel technology that delivers superior image quality through several innovations: backside illumination (BSI) for improved light sensitivity; deep trench isolation (DTI) for reduced crosstalk and improved color fidelity; and advanced microlens technology for better light collection. PureCel sensors offer excellent low-light performance, high dynamic range, and reduced noise compared to traditional FSI sensors. The technology enables smaller pixel sizes while maintaining image quality, allowing higher resolution in compact form factors.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring excellent image quality in challenging lighting, choose PureCel technology sensors. Contact us for specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for Will Semiconductor image sensors?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor image sensors support multiple interface options to suit different system architectures. MIPI CSI-2 is the most common interface for mobile and automotive applications, supporting high data rates up to 2.5Gbps per lane. Parallel DVP interface is available for simpler systems and legacy applications. LVDS interface is offered for high-speed industrial applications. Some sensors also support sub-LVDS or HiSPi interfaces. The number of MIPI lanes (1-4) can be configured based on bandwidth requirements. All interfaces include standard synchronization signals and support various data formats including RAW10, RAW12, and YUV.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select interface based on your processor capabilities and bandwidth requirements. MIPI CSI-2 is recommended for most new designs.

faqs.question: What automotive qualifications do Will Semiconductor sensors have?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers AEC-Q100 qualified image sensors for automotive applications. These sensors meet stringent automotive reliability requirements including extended temperature range (-40°C to +105°C), high humidity operation, and mechanical stress resistance. Many automotive sensors also support functional safety requirements up to ASIL-B level. Features specifically designed for automotive include LED flicker mitigation for traffic sign recognition, high dynamic range (HDR) for challenging lighting conditions, and global shutter options for motion capture. The automotive portfolio covers surround view, ADAS, in-cabin monitoring, and e-mirror applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For automotive applications, specify AEC-Q100 qualified parts. Contact our automotive team for functional safety documentation.

faqs.question: How do I evaluate image sensor performance for my application?
faqs.answer: Evaluating image sensor performance requires considering multiple factors: resolution and sharpness measured using MTF charts; low-light sensitivity measured in lux and signal-to-noise ratio; dynamic range for handling high-contrast scenes; color accuracy measured against standard color charts; and power consumption for battery-operated devices. Will Semiconductor provides evaluation kits with reference lenses and software tools for image quality analysis. Key performance metrics include SNR10 (signal-to-noise ratio at 10 lux), dynamic range in dB, and dark current specifications. Our FAE team can assist with evaluation setup and performance analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request evaluation kits and reference designs for hands-on testing. Contact our imaging specialists for evaluation guidance.

products.name: 50MP CMOS Image Sensor
products.shortDescription: 50MP 1/1.55-inch optical format CMOS sensor with 1.0um pixels, PureCel Plus technology, and 8K video support for flagship smartphones.
descriptionParagraphs: The OV50A40 is a high-performance 50MP CMOS image sensor featuring advanced PureCel Plus technology for exceptional image quality.
With 1.0um pixels and a large 1/1.55-inch optical format, it delivers outstanding low-light performance and high dynamic range.
The sensor supports 8K video recording at 30fps and 4K at 120fps, making it ideal for flagship smartphone camera systems.
features: PureCel Plus technology for superior image quality
8K video recording at 30 frames per second
MIPI CSI-2 interface with 4 data lanes

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Imaging Systems
faeReview.content: The OV50A40 represents the pinnacle of mobile imaging technology. I've worked with this sensor in multiple flagship smartphone designs and the image quality is exceptional. The PureCel Plus technology delivers noticeably better low-light performance compared to previous generation sensors. The 1.0um pixels strike an excellent balance between resolution and light sensitivity. The QPD autofocus is fast and accurate, even in challenging lighting conditions. For high-end mobile applications, this sensor offers the best combination of resolution, performance, and features currently available.
faeReview.highlight: Exceptional image quality with PureCel Plus technology

alternativeParts.reason: Larger pixels and sensor size for better low-light performance
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications prioritizing low-light over maximum resolution

companionParts.description: Low-noise LDO for sensor analog power supply
Evaluation kit with reference lens and software
Voice coil motor driver for autofocus
Reference lens module for OV50A40

faqs.question: What is the maximum video resolution supported by OV50A40?
faqs.answer: The OV50A40 supports maximum 8K video recording at 30 frames per second (8192 x 6144 resolution). It also supports 4K video at up to 120fps for slow-motion capture, 1080p at 240fps, and 720p at 480fps. The sensor uses MIPI CSI-2 interface with 4 data lanes to handle the high data bandwidth required for 8K video. Video modes support both HDR and non-HDR operation with various output formats including RAW10 and RAW12.
faqs.decisionGuide: For 8K video applications, ensure your processor supports MIPI CSI-2 4-lane at 2.5Gbps per lane. Contact us for interface timing details.

faqs.question: What autofocus technology does OV50A40 support?
faqs.answer: The OV50A40 features Quad Phase Detection (QPD) autofocus technology. QPD uses on-chip phase detection pixels arranged in a quad pattern to enable fast and accurate autofocus in both horizontal and vertical directions. This provides superior autofocus performance compared to traditional dual-pixel PDAF, especially for off-center subjects. The sensor supports both open-loop and closed-loop VCM (Voice Coil Motor) control for precise lens positioning. Autofocus works effectively even in low-light conditions down to 1 lux.
faqs.decisionGuide: For fast autofocus applications, pair OV50A40 with a high-quality VCM driver and lens module. Contact us for recommended companion components.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of OV50A40?
faqs.answer: The OV50A40 power consumption varies by operating mode: typical active power is 350-450mW at full resolution 30fps; 8K video mode consumes approximately 400mW; 4K video at 60fps consumes around 300mW; and standby power is less than 1mW. Power consumption scales with frame rate and resolution. The sensor supports multiple power modes including active, standby, and sleep for power management. Proper power supply sequencing is required for reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design power supply with adequate margin (600mW recommended). Use switching regulator for DVDD and LDO for AVDD. Contact us for power supply design guidance.

faqs.question: What lens options are available for OV50A40?
faqs.answer: The OV50A40 uses standard CSP (Chip Scale Package) with optical center alignment features. Compatible lenses are available from multiple suppliers including Sunny Optical, Largan Precision, and Kantatsu. Lens selection depends on application requirements: wide-angle lenses (120-130° FOV) for main cameras; ultra-wide lenses (150°+) for ultra-wide modules; and telephoto lenses for zoom applications. The large 1/1.55-inch optical format requires lenses designed for this sensor size. Will Semiconductor provides reference lens designs and can recommend qualified lens suppliers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for reference lens designs and qualified lens supplier recommendations for your specific application.

faqs.question: Does OV50A40 support HDR imaging?
faqs.answer: Yes, the OV50A40 supports multiple HDR modes including staggered HDR and DCG (Dual Conversion Gain) HDR. Staggered HDR captures multiple exposures in sequence for high dynamic range scenes. DCG HDR uses dual conversion gain technology to simultaneously capture high and low gain images for real-time HDR processing. The sensor supports HDR video up to 4K resolution. HDR modes can be combined with other features like QPD autofocus. The HDR implementation provides up to 100dB dynamic range for challenging lighting conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For HDR applications, ensure your ISP supports the HDR output format. Contact us for HDR tuning guidelines and reference settings.

products.name: 3MP Automotive CMOS Sensor
products.shortDescription: 3MP 1/2.44-inch automotive CMOS sensor with LED flicker mitigation, HDR, and ASIL-B support for surround view and ADAS cameras.
descriptionParagraphs: The OX03F10 is a high-performance 3MP automotive-grade CMOS image sensor designed for surround view and ADAS applications.
Featuring advanced LED flicker mitigation (LFM) technology, it eliminates flickering from LED traffic lights and signs for reliable machine vision.
The sensor is AEC-Q100 Grade 2 qualified and supports ASIL-B functional safety, making it ideal for safety-critical automotive camera systems.

faeReview.author: David Park
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Systems
faeReview.content: The OX03F10 is an excellent choice for automotive camera applications. I've specified this sensor in multiple surround view and ADAS projects. The LED flicker mitigation is critical for traffic sign recognition - without it, LED signs appear to flicker or disappear entirely. The 3.0um pixels provide excellent low-light sensitivity for nighttime driving. ASIL-B support simplifies functional safety certification. The HDR performance handles the challenging contrast between bright sky and dark road. For automotive applications requiring reliable machine vision, this sensor delivers consistent performance.
faeReview.highlight: Essential LED flicker mitigation for automotive safety

alternativeParts.useCase: Rear view and basic surround view cameras

alternativeParts.reason: Higher resolution for advanced ADAS applications
alternativeParts.useCase: High-resolution ADAS and surround view systems

companionParts.description: GMSL2 deserializer for long cable automotive applications
Automotive evaluation kit with reference design
Automotive Ethernet PHY for camera connectivity
Automotive PMIC for camera module power

faqs.question: What is LED flicker mitigation and why is it important?
faqs.answer: LED flicker mitigation (LFM) is a technology that eliminates the flickering effect when capturing LED traffic lights and signs. Standard cameras capture images at specific intervals, and if the LED pulse frequency doesn't match the camera timing, the LED appears to flicker or may be completely missed in some frames. This is dangerous for ADAS systems that rely on traffic sign recognition. LFM technology uses extended exposure timing to capture the complete LED pulse cycle, ensuring consistent visibility. The OX03F10's LFM handles pulse frequencies from 90Hz to over 2kHz, covering all common LED types.
faqs.decisionGuide: For any automotive camera application involving traffic sign recognition, LFM is essential. Contact us for LFM implementation guidelines.

faqs.question: What functional safety features does OX03F10 support?
faqs.answer: The OX03F10 supports ASIL-B functional safety level according to ISO 26262. Safety features include: comprehensive built-in self-test (BIST) for sensor diagnostics; CRC protection for register settings and output data; temperature monitoring with alarm thresholds; and output data tagging for frame identification. The sensor provides diagnostic information through dedicated registers that can be monitored by the system safety monitor. These features enable the sensor to be used in safety-critical applications like automatic emergency braking and lane departure warning systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: For ASIL-B applications, implement all recommended safety mechanisms. Contact our automotive team for functional safety documentation.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of OX03F10?
faqs.answer: The OX03F10 is qualified to AEC-Q100 Grade 2, supporting operating temperatures from -40°C to +105°C. This wide temperature range is essential for automotive applications where cameras are mounted outside the vehicle and exposed to extreme environmental conditions. The sensor maintains consistent image quality across the entire temperature range through built-in temperature compensation. Dark current performance is optimized for high-temperature operation common in engine compartment or roof-mounted camera locations. The package is designed for automotive reliability requirements including thermal cycling and mechanical stress.
faqs.decisionGuide: For Grade 2 applications, ensure proper thermal management in your camera module design. Contact us for thermal design guidelines.

faqs.question: What HDR modes does OX03F10 support?
faqs.answer: The OX03F10 supports multiple HDR modes: 120dB HDR using staggered exposure for most applications; 140dB ultra-high dynamic range mode for extreme contrast scenes; and split-pixel HDR using dual-diode technology for motion artifact reduction. The HDR modes can be combined with LED flicker mitigation for challenging automotive lighting conditions. The sensor supports both line-interleaved HDR output for real-time processing and frame-sequential HDR for maximum dynamic range. HDR can be enabled for both video streaming and single-frame capture modes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select HDR mode based on your scene contrast requirements. 120dB mode is suitable for most applications. Contact us for HDR tuning recommendations.

faqs.question: What lens recommendations do you have for OX03F10?
faqs.answer: The OX03F10 uses automotive-grade CSP packaging compatible with standard M12 lens mounts. For surround view applications, 190-degree fisheye lenses are typically used to provide 360-degree coverage with four cameras. For ADAS front cameras, lenses with 50-60 degree horizontal FOV are common. The 1/2.44-inch optical format requires lenses designed for 1/2.5-inch or larger sensors. Automotive lenses must meet strict reliability requirements including vibration resistance, thermal stability, and IP67 sealing. Will Semiconductor works with qualified automotive lens suppliers including Sunny Optical and Sekonix.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for qualified automotive lens supplier recommendations and reference lens designs for your specific application.

categories.name: Power Management ICs
categories.description: High-efficiency power management solutions including DC-DC converters, LDO regulators, battery management ICs, and LED drivers for portable and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription: Will Semiconductor's power management portfolio delivers comprehensive solutions for efficient power conversion and distribution. The product range includes high-efficiency DC-DC buck and boost converters for voltage regulation; low-noise LDO linear regulators for sensitive analog circuits; battery management ICs for charging and protection of Li-ion and Li-polymer batteries; and LED drivers for display backlighting and general lighting. These products feature wide input voltage ranges, high efficiency, and compact packages suitable for space-constrained applications. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides power architecture design support, thermal analysis, and BOM optimization services.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Will Semiconductor Power Management ICs
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for choosing the right power management solution

faqs.question: What types of DC-DC converters does Will Semiconductor offer?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers a comprehensive range of DC-DC converters including synchronous buck converters for step-down voltage regulation; boost converters for step-up applications; and buck-boost converters for applications where input voltage can be above or below the output. These converters support input voltages from 2.5V to 60V and output currents up to 10A. Features include high switching frequencies (up to 3MHz) for compact designs, programmable soft-start, and comprehensive protection features. The portfolio includes both integrated solutions with internal power switches and controllers for external FET configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select converter type based on your input/output voltage relationship and current requirements. Contact us for power architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I choose between switching regulators and LDOs?
faqs.answer: The choice between switching regulators (DC-DC) and LDOs depends on your application requirements. Switching regulators offer higher efficiency (typically 85-95%) and can step up or step down voltage, but generate more noise and require external inductors. LDOs provide clean output with very low noise and no switching ripple, but are less efficient when input voltage is much higher than output. Use switching regulators for high current applications or when efficiency is critical. Use LDOs for noise-sensitive analog circuits or when input-output voltage difference is small. Will Semiconductor offers both types with various performance characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: For noise-sensitive applications, consider LDOs. For battery-powered devices, switching regulators are preferred. Contact us for power architecture guidance.

faqs.question: What battery management solutions are available?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor provides comprehensive battery management solutions including single-cell and multi-cell Li-ion/Li-polymer battery chargers; battery protection ICs for overcharge, overdischarge, and overcurrent protection; fuel gauge ICs for accurate battery capacity monitoring; and power path management ICs for seamless switching between battery and external power. These products support various battery chemistries and configurations, with charge currents ranging from 100mA to 5A. Safety features include temperature monitoring, safety timers, and JEITA-compliant charging profiles.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select battery management ICs based on your battery chemistry, cell count, and charge current requirements. Contact us for battery management design support.

faqs.question: What LED driver options does Will Semiconductor provide?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers LED drivers for various applications including white LED drivers for LCD backlighting; RGB LED drivers for status indication and decorative lighting; and high-brightness LED drivers for flashlight and camera flash applications. Topologies include charge pumps for low-current applications; inductive boost converters for higher currents; and linear drivers for simple, low-cost solutions. Features include PWM dimming control, analog dimming, and automatic brightness adjustment. These drivers support series and parallel LED configurations with currents up to 1.5A per channel.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select LED driver based on your LED configuration, current requirements, and dimming method. Contact us for LED driver recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of Will Semiconductor DC-DC converters?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor DC-DC converters achieve high efficiency through synchronous rectification and advanced control algorithms. Typical efficiency ranges from 85% to 95% depending on input/output voltage ratio and load current. Peak efficiency is usually achieved at 50-80% of rated load. Light-load efficiency is optimized through PFM (Pulse Frequency Modulation) or burst mode operation. The efficiency curves are characterized across the full operating range and are available in datasheets. For battery-powered applications, high efficiency directly translates to longer battery life.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review efficiency curves in datasheets for your specific operating conditions. Contact us for efficiency optimization recommendations.

products.name: 300mA Ultra-Low Noise LDO
products.shortDescription: 300mA low-dropout linear regulator with ultra-low noise (6uVrms) and high PSRR (80dB at 1kHz) for RF and analog power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: The WL2831D is a high-performance 300mA LDO regulator designed for noise-sensitive applications such as RF circuits and precision analog systems.
With ultra-low output noise of only 6uVrms and high power supply rejection ratio of 80dB at 1kHz, it provides clean power for sensitive circuits.
The device features a wide input voltage range of 1.7V to 5.5V and is available in fixed and adjustable output voltage versions.

faeReview.author: James Wilson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Management
faeReview.content: The WL2831D is my go-to recommendation for powering noise-sensitive circuits. The 6uVrms noise figure is among the best in its class. I've used this LDO in camera module designs where clean analog power is critical for image quality. The high PSRR effectively filters switching noise from upstream DC-DC converters. The dropout voltage is reasonable for a low-noise design. For RF applications, the noise performance ensures minimal impact on receiver sensitivity. The SOT-23-5 package is convenient for compact designs. Overall, this LDO offers excellent performance at a competitive price point.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-low noise performance for sensitive analog circuits

companionParts.description: Step-down converter for efficient pre-regulation
CMOS sensor for camera applications
Power distribution switch with soft-start
Evaluation board with test points

faqs.question: What is the dropout voltage of WL2831D?
faqs.answer: The WL2831D features a low dropout voltage of typically 120mV at 300mA load current when Vout is 3.3V. Dropout voltage scales with output current - at 150mA it is approximately 60mV, and at 50mA it is about 20mV. The dropout voltage also varies slightly with output voltage setting. This low dropout allows the LDO to maintain regulation even when input voltage is close to output voltage, maximizing battery life in portable applications. The dropout characteristic is fully specified over temperature in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your minimum input voltage is at least dropout voltage plus output voltage. Contact us for dropout calculations under your specific conditions.

faqs.question: How does the PSRR of WL2831D compare to other LDOs?
faqs.answer: The WL2831D offers excellent PSRR (Power Supply Rejection Ratio) performance: 80dB at 1kHz, 70dB at 10kHz, 60dB at 100kHz, and 50dB at 1MHz. This high PSRR effectively attenuates ripple and noise from the upstream power supply, making it ideal for sensitive analog and RF circuits. Compared to general-purpose LDOs which typically offer 40-60dB PSRR at 1kHz, the WL2831D provides significantly better noise rejection. The PSRR performance is maintained across the operating voltage and current range.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications sensitive to power supply noise, WL2831D's high PSRR provides superior performance. Contact us for PSRR measurement data.

faqs.question: What packages are available for WL2831D?
faqs.answer: The WL2831D is available in two package options: SOT-23-5 (2.9mm x 1.6mm) for general applications; and DFN-6 (2mm x 2mm) for space-constrained designs. Both packages have identical electrical performance. The SOT-23-5 is easier to handle and inspect during manufacturing. The DFN-6 offers smaller footprint and better thermal performance due to exposed pad. Package selection depends on space constraints and manufacturing capabilities. Both packages are RoHS compliant and lead-free.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SOT-23-5 for ease of manufacturing or DFN-6 for compact designs. Contact us for package thermal characteristics.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of WL2831D?
faqs.answer: The WL2831D thermal performance depends on package type and PCB layout. The SOT-23-5 package has thermal resistance (Theta-JA) of approximately 200°C/W on standard PCB. The DFN-6 with exposed pad achieves better thermal performance of about 80°C/W with proper copper area. Maximum power dissipation is limited by junction temperature (125°C maximum). For example, at 3.6V input and 1.8V output at 300mA, power dissipation is 540mW. With SOT-23-5, temperature rise is approximately 108°C, requiring careful thermal management. The DFN-6 would have about 43°C rise under same conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high power dissipation applications, use DFN-6 package with adequate copper area. Contact us for thermal design recommendations.

faqs.question: Does WL2831D support adjustable output voltage?
faqs.answer: Yes, the WL2831D is available in both fixed output voltage versions (1.2V, 1.5V, 1.8V, 2.5V, 2.8V, 3.0V, 3.3V) and an adjustable version. The adjustable version uses external resistor divider to set output voltage from 0.8V to 4.5V. The feedback voltage is 0.8V typical. Fixed voltage versions offer better accuracy (±1%) and require fewer external components. Adjustable version provides flexibility for non-standard voltages. Both versions have identical electrical characteristics and protection features.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use fixed voltage for standard rails to minimize BOM. Use adjustable version for custom voltages. Contact us for resistor divider calculations.

products.name: 2A Synchronous Buck Converter
products.shortDescription: 2A synchronous step-down DC-DC converter with 2.5V-5.5V input, adjustable output, and 3MHz switching frequency for compact designs.
descriptionParagraphs: The WL2801 is a high-efficiency 2A synchronous buck converter designed for portable and battery-powered applications.
With a wide input voltage range of 2.5V to 5.5V and adjustable output from 0.6V to VIN, it can power a variety of loads from single-cell Li-ion batteries or 5V rails.
The 3MHz switching frequency allows the use of small inductors and capacitors, minimizing PCB area and solution cost.
features: High efficiency: up to 95% at 3.3V to 1.8V conversion
100% duty cycle for low dropout operation
Current limit and thermal shutdown protection

faeReview.author: Robert Lee
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Power Electronics
faeReview.content: The WL2801 is an excellent choice for battery-powered applications requiring high efficiency. The 3MHz switching frequency is higher than many competitors, allowing smaller external components. I've achieved solution sizes under 25mm² including inductor and capacitors. The efficiency peaks at 95% for typical 3.3V to 1.8V conversion, which is impressive for this current range. The 40uA quiescent current helps extend battery life in standby. The internal compensation simplifies design - no need to calculate external compensation networks. For compact, efficient power conversion, this buck converter delivers excellent performance.

alternativeParts.reason: Higher current capability for demanding applications

companionParts.description: Post-regulation for noise-sensitive rails
1.0uH inductor for WL2801
10uF ceramic capacitor for output
Evaluation board with efficiency measurements

faqs.question: What inductor value is recommended for WL2801?
faqs.answer: The WL2801 operates at 3MHz switching frequency, allowing small inductor values. For most applications, a 1.0uH inductor is recommended. This provides good balance between ripple current, transient response, and physical size. The inductor should have saturation current rating at least 20% higher than maximum load current (2.4A minimum for 2A load). Recommended inductors include 2.0mm x 1.6mm chip inductors for compact designs or 3.0mm x 3.0mm inductors for lowest DCR and highest efficiency. Inductor selection affects output ripple, transient response, and overall efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1.0uH inductor with >2.4A saturation current. Contact us for recommended inductor part numbers.

faqs.question: What is the efficiency of WL2801 at light loads?
faqs.answer: The WL2801 maintains good efficiency across the load range. At heavy loads (1-2A), efficiency is 90-95% depending on input/output voltages. At medium loads (100-500mA), efficiency is typically 85-92%. At light loads (10-50mA), the converter automatically enters PFM (Pulse Frequency Modulation) mode to maintain efficiency above 70%. The 40uA quiescent current helps maintain efficiency at very light loads. For battery-powered applications, this efficiency profile maximizes battery life across various usage patterns.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery applications, efficiency at your typical load current is most important. Contact us for efficiency curves at your operating points.

faqs.question: What output capacitor is recommended for WL2801?
faqs.answer: The WL2801 requires output capacitance of at least 10uF for stable operation. Ceramic capacitors with X5R or X7R dielectric are recommended for best performance. The capacitor should be rated for at least 10V and placed close to the IC pins. For applications with large load transients, additional capacitance (22-47uF) may be beneficial. Low ESR ceramic capacitors provide lowest output ripple. The output capacitor affects loop stability, transient response, and output ripple voltage. Avoid tantalum or electrolytic capacitors as primary output capacitance due to higher ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10uF X5R/X7R ceramic capacitor minimum. Add more capacitance for large load transients. Contact us for capacitor recommendations.

faqs.question: Does WL2801 support 100% duty cycle operation?
faqs.answer: Yes, the WL2801 supports 100% duty cycle operation, which means the high-side switch can remain on continuously. This allows the output voltage to approach the input voltage minus the RDS(on) drop of the high-side MOSFET. At 100% duty cycle, the converter operates in dropout mode similar to an LDO. This feature is important for battery-powered applications where the battery voltage drops close to the required output voltage. The transition to 100% duty cycle is automatic and seamless.
faqs.decisionGuide: 100% duty cycle maximizes battery life by extracting all available energy. Contact us for dropout voltage characteristics.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency of WL2801 and can it be adjusted?
faqs.answer: The WL2801 operates at a fixed 3MHz switching frequency. This frequency is internally set and cannot be adjusted externally. The 3MHz frequency was selected as an optimal balance between solution size, efficiency, and EMI. Higher frequencies allow smaller components but reduce efficiency. Lower frequencies improve efficiency but require larger inductors and capacitors. The fixed frequency simplifies design - no external frequency setting components are needed. The frequency remains constant across load and voltage conditions, making EMI filtering predictable.
faqs.decisionGuide: The fixed 3MHz frequency is suitable for most applications. Contact us if your application requires different switching frequency.

categories.name: Signal Chain Products
categories.description: Precision analog and mixed-signal products including analog switches, interface ICs, operational amplifiers, and level translators for signal conditioning.
categories.longDescription: Will Semiconductor's signal chain portfolio provides high-performance analog and mixed-signal solutions for signal conditioning, routing, and interface applications. The product range includes low-on-resistance analog switches for signal routing; high-speed and precision operational amplifiers for signal conditioning; level translators for mixed-voltage systems; and interface ICs for various communication standards. These products feature low power consumption, high bandwidth, and excellent linearity for demanding applications. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides signal chain design support, simulation models, and reference designs.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Will Semiconductor Signal Chain Products
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for choosing signal chain solutions

faqs.question: What types of analog switches does Will Semiconductor offer?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers a comprehensive range of analog switches including SPST, SPDT, SP4T, and SP8T configurations; low-voltage switches for battery-powered applications; high-bandwidth switches for video and high-speed signals; and low-charge injection switches for precision applications. On-resistance ranges from 0.3Ω to 50Ω depending on configuration. These switches operate from 1.8V to 12V supplies and are available in compact packages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select switch based on number of channels, on-resistance, bandwidth, and voltage requirements. Contact us for switch selection guidance.

faqs.question: What operational amplifiers are available?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor provides various operational amplifiers including general-purpose op-amps for standard applications; low-power op-amps for battery-operated devices; precision op-amps with low offset voltage; and high-speed op-amps for fast signal processing. Features include rail-to-rail input/output, low noise, and wide bandwidth options. These op-amps support supply voltages from 1.8V to 36V and are available in single, dual, and quad configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose op-amp based on bandwidth, precision, power consumption, and package requirements. Contact us for op-amp recommendations.

products.name: Quad SPST Analog Switch
products.shortDescription: Quad SPST analog switch with 0.5Ω on-resistance, 1.8V-5.5V operation, and high bandwidth for signal routing applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The WS4664 is a quad SPST (Single Pole Single Throw) analog switch designed for signal routing in portable and battery-powered applications.
With ultra-low on-resistance of 0.5Ω typical and wide bandwidth of 300MHz, it provides excellent signal integrity for audio and data signals.
The device operates from 1.8V to 5.5V supplies and features break-before-make switching to prevent signal contention.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Analog Design
faeReview.content: The WS4664 is an excellent choice for signal routing applications requiring low on-resistance. The 0.5Ω Ron minimizes signal attenuation and distortion. I've used this switch in audio routing applications where the low charge injection prevents audible pops. The 300MHz bandwidth handles high-speed digital signals without degradation. The wide voltage range allows use in both 3.3V and 5V systems. For general-purpose analog switching, this quad switch offers great value and performance.
faeReview.highlight: Low on-resistance with high bandwidth for versatile switching

companionParts.description: Precision op-amp for signal conditioning
Low-noise power supply for analog circuits

faqs.question: What is the on-resistance of WS4664?
faqs.answer: The WS4664 features ultra-low on-resistance of 0.5Ω typical at 3.3V supply with 5V control voltage. On-resistance varies slightly with supply voltage and signal voltage. At 1.8V supply, typical on-resistance is 0.8Ω. The on-resistance remains flat across the signal range, ensuring consistent performance. Low on-resistance minimizes signal attenuation and distortion, making it ideal for audio and precision applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications sensitive to signal loss, WS4664's low Ron provides excellent performance. Contact us for on-resistance characteristics across voltage and temperature.

products.name: Precision Operational Amplifier
products.shortDescription: Precision op-amp with 50uV offset, 2MHz bandwidth, and rail-to-rail input/output for sensor signal conditioning.
descriptionParagraphs: The WS3210 is a precision operational amplifier featuring low offset voltage and rail-to-rail input/output operation.
With 50uV typical input offset voltage and 2MHz gain-bandwidth product, it provides accurate amplification for sensor signals.
The device operates from 2.1V to 5.5V supplies and consumes only 150uA per amplifier, making it ideal for battery-powered applications.

faeReview.author: David Park
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Precision Analog
faeReview.content: The WS3210 offers excellent precision at a competitive price. The 50uV offset is suitable for most sensor applications without external calibration. I've used this op-amp in temperature measurement circuits where accuracy is critical. The rail-to-rail input allows sensing near supply rails. The low power consumption extends battery life in portable equipment. For general-purpose precision amplification, this op-amp delivers reliable performance.
faeReview.highlight: Precision performance with low power consumption

companionParts.description: Signal routing for multi-channel systems
Clean power supply for analog circuits

faqs.question: What is the offset voltage of WS3210?
faqs.answer: The WS3210 features low input offset voltage of 50uV typical, with maximum 500uV over temperature and process variations. This low offset eliminates the need for external calibration in many applications. The offset drift is typically 0.5uV/°C, ensuring stable performance across temperature. For precision DC applications, this offset performance is excellent in this price range.
faqs.decisionGuide: For precision DC applications, WS3210's low offset provides accurate amplification. Contact us for offset voltage distribution data.

categories.name: RF and Connectivity
categories.description: RF front-end modules, Bluetooth Low Energy solutions, and connectivity ICs for wireless communication in IoT and consumer electronics.
categories.longDescription: Will Semiconductor's RF and connectivity portfolio enables wireless communication for IoT devices, wearables, and consumer electronics. The product range includes RF front-end modules with integrated power amplifiers, filters, and switches; Bluetooth Low Energy transceivers for low-power wireless connectivity; and various connectivity ICs for wireless applications. These products feature low power consumption, high integration, and compact packages suitable for space-constrained designs. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo provides RF design support, antenna recommendations, and regulatory compliance guidance.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Will Semiconductor RF Products
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide for choosing RF and connectivity solutions

faqs.question: What RF front-end modules does Will Semiconductor offer?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor provides integrated RF front-end modules for various wireless standards including Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and cellular applications. These modules integrate power amplifiers, low-noise amplifiers, filters, and switches in compact packages. Features include high linearity, excellent efficiency, and comprehensive matching networks. The modules simplify RF design and reduce time-to-market.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select RF front-end module based on your wireless standard and power requirements. Contact us for RF design support.

faqs.question: What Bluetooth solutions are available?
faqs.answer: Will Semiconductor offers Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) solutions including transceivers and SoC devices. These products support Bluetooth 5.0 and 5.1 standards with features like long range, high speed, and direction finding. Power consumption is optimized for battery-operated devices with sleep currents below 1uA. The devices include integrated ARM Cortex-M processors for application processing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose BLE solution based on range, data rate, and processing requirements. Contact us for Bluetooth design guidance.

products.name: 2.4GHz RF Front-End Module
products.shortDescription: 2.4GHz RF front-end module with integrated PA, LNA, and switch for Bluetooth and Zigbee applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The WS2400 is a highly integrated 2.4GHz RF front-end module designed for Bluetooth, Zigbee, and ISM band applications.
It integrates a power amplifier with 22dBm output power, low-noise amplifier with 2dB NF, and T/R switch in a compact QFN package.
The module includes input and output matching networks, simplifying RF design and reducing external component count.

faeReview.author: James Wilson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - RF Systems
faeReview.content: The WS2400 simplifies 2.4GHz RF design significantly. The integrated matching networks save weeks of tuning time. The 22dBm output power provides excellent range for Bluetooth applications. The 2dB noise figure ensures good receiver sensitivity. I've used this FEM in multiple IoT projects where it reduced RF design risk. The QFN package is easy to assemble and provides good thermal performance. For 2.4GHz wireless applications, this FEM offers excellent integration and performance.
faeReview.highlight: High integration with excellent RF performance

companionParts.description: Nordic BLE SoC for wireless applications
Efficient power supply for RF circuits

faqs.question: What is the output power of WS2400?
faqs.answer: The WS2400 provides 22dBm (158mW) output power at the antenna port when operating from 3.3V supply. This high output power enables long-range Bluetooth and Zigbee communication. The power amplifier includes automatic gain control and temperature compensation. Output power can be reduced through external control for shorter range applications or regulatory compliance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For long-range applications, WS2400's 22dBm output provides excellent range. Contact us for range estimation based on your antenna and environment.

products.name: Bluetooth 5.0 Low Energy SoC
products.shortDescription: Bluetooth 5.0 LE SoC with ARM Cortex-M4, 512KB Flash, and ultra-low power consumption for IoT applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The WS8226 is a highly integrated Bluetooth 5.0 Low Energy System-on-Chip featuring an ARM Cortex-M4 processor.
With 512KB Flash memory and 64KB RAM, it provides ample resources for complex IoT applications and protocol stacks.
The device achieves ultra-low power consumption with 3mA TX current at 0dBm and 1uA sleep current with RAM retention.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Systems
faeReview.content: The WS8226 offers excellent integration for BLE IoT applications. The ARM Cortex-M4 provides sufficient processing power for most applications without an external MCU. The 1uA sleep current with RAM retention is impressive for battery-operated devices. Bluetooth 5.0 features like long range and 2Mbps data rate enable new use cases. The integrated balun reduces external component count. For BLE IoT designs, this SoC provides a complete solution with minimal external components.
faeReview.highlight: Complete BLE solution with ultra-low power consumption

alternativeParts.reason: Similar capability from established BLE supplier

companionParts.description: External PA for extended range applications
Efficient power supply for SoC

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of WS8226?
faqs.answer: The WS8226 achieves industry-leading power consumption: 3mA TX current at 0dBm output power; 3.5mA RX current; 4.5mA in active mode with radio off; and 1uA in deep sleep with RAM retention. This ultra-low power consumption enables years of operation from coin cell batteries. The device includes various power modes to optimize consumption for different application scenarios.
faqs.decisionGuide: For battery-operated IoT devices, WS8226's low power extends battery life significantly. Contact us for battery life estimation.
